music_notes: Live jazz, swing and big band acts — rotating artists
description: St James's institution since 1929. Seven nights of live music a week — Friday and Saturday evenings feature jazz, swing and brass bands in the grand Art Deco dining room. One of the most reliably fun evenings in Central London.

music_notes: Rotating headline jazz acts — serious programming
price_notes: Free with dining, tickets for Sunday lunch
description: Jools Holland is patron. The Belgravia original of the Boisdale group — whisky bar, cigars, and nightly live jazz from serious musicians. Late licence, proper food, properly programmed music. One of the few places in London where dinner jazz means actual jazz.
The City outpost of Boisdale — same serious jazz programming as Belgravia, in the heart of Canary Wharf. Steak, whisky and live jazz every evening. The Sunday lunch is ticketed with headline acts.

music_notes: Cabaret and jazz — Crazy Coqs upstairs, brasserie dining below
price_notes: £10–£25 for Crazy Coqs; free in brasserie
description: Spectacular 1930s Art Deco brasserie beneath Piccadilly — one of London's most beautiful rooms. Crazy Coqs upstairs is a proper cabaret and jazz venue with ticketed shows. The brasserie itself has occasional jazz nights at extremely reasonable prices.

music_notes: Neal Richardson — resident pianist and vocalist
description: Neal Richardson — one of the UK's most accomplished pianist-vocalists — plays Tuesday to Thursday afternoons in the most prestigious setting in London: the Old War Office, Whitehall. Afternoon tea in a room that Winston Churchill once walked through. Jazz doesn't get more civilised.

Glamorous old-school Theatreland seafood restaurant with a resident jazz duo every Sunday. Champagne, Atlantic prawns and live standards in one of London's most beautifully preserved dining rooms. Been serving fish since 1896.
London's oldest restaurant (est. 1798) with occasional jazz evenings in its Victorian dining rooms. Not a regular programme — check the website. Worth it when they run them purely for the atmosphere.

price_notes: Free with dining — restaurant is pricey
description: Celebrity haunt in a converted Victorian fire station. Jazz duo plays Friday and Saturday evenings in the bar and restaurant. The music is good even if the crowd is more interested in being seen than listening.

music_notes: Live jazz and band — members and dining guests
description: London's most famous private members club — live jazz and bands every Thursday to Saturday. Accessible to non-members who book the restaurant. The most glamorous setting for jazz in London, if you can get in.

South London's only dedicated jazz restaurant — French bistro cooking alongside a genuine jazz programme. Intimate room, knowledgeable crowd, serious artists. One of the best nights out in SE London and consistently underrated.

music_notes: Eclectic programme — jazz, folk, world music
description: Intimate Camden café-venue with serious musical programming. Vegetarian food, BYOB wine, 50-seat capacity. The kind of place where you sit two feet from the musicians. Not exclusively jazz but frequently excellent jazz.

frequency: Every Sunday + occasional Candlelight & Keys evenings
music_notes: Sunday jazz sessions — vocal jazz, Buena Vista rhythms, New Orleans swing. Candlelight & Keys evenings: lights out, fire cooking, menu built around the performing artist
description: Named after chef-owner Robin Gill's father — a jazz trumpeter who played with Count Basie. Sunday sessions are a tribute to his performances. Eclectic programme: smooth vocal jazz, Buena Vista-style rhythms, New Orleans swing alongside Carlingford oysters, smoked-eel gildas and beef-shin pie. The Candlelight & Keys evenings (in partnership with Kawai) are something else entirely: lights out, fire cooking, the whole menu built around the artist.

music_notes: Live jazz from respected names — Zhenya Strigalev, Jean Toussaint and others
description: Friday nights at Pollini pulse with live jazz from serious musicians — Zhenya Strigalev and Jean Toussaint have both played. 42,000 sq ft of grade II listed former automobile factory with its own Steinway and state-of-the-art sound. The £54 two-course menu runs from datterini pizzetta to braised beef cheeks with Umbrian black truffle. Loïc Le Gaillard has built something genuinely distinctive here.

music_notes: Pianist playing bebop and ragtime at the long curved bar — may be co-owner James Dye's father
description: Beyond the burgundy velvet curtains, Joy Yamusangie's bold jazz-inspired artworks line the walls — matched at weekends by a pianist working through bebop and ragtime at the long curved bar. Martinis, a house cheeseburger au poivre and New York cheesecake. The kind of place that makes you feel like you're in the Village Vanguard neighbourhood.

music_notes: Serious jazz programme — regulars include Yohan Kebede of Kokoroko
description: Serious jazz programme draws in regulars such as Yohan Kebede of Kokoroko, making it a known hub on London's jazz circuit. Japanese menu — tuna sushi, gyozas, pork tonkatsu — alongside cocktails and natural wines. You're really there for the music.

frequency: Jazz in the Cellars — winter and spring season
music_notes: Jazz in the Cellars — curated by Mark Kavuma (Banger Factory Records)
description: Jazz in the Cellars runs through winter and spring, curated by trumpeter Mark Kavuma — founder of Banger Factory Records and one of the most vital figures on the British jazz scene. Georgian townhouse restaurant in Vauxhall with Jackson Boxer's famous Dexter beef tartare and an exceptional wine list. One of the most thoughtfully programmed jazz dining experiences in London.

music_notes: Free jazz groove sessions — Pharoah Russell with London's most exciting jazz musicians
description: Every Wednesday Pharoah Russell convenes some of London's most exciting jazz musicians for the free jazz groove sessions — soulful vocals, deep bass and tight grooves. More bar than restaurant but highballs, flatbreads and skewers make a convincing case for staying all night. The basement beneath Stone Nest arts venue on Shaftesbury Avenue.
